perspectives.raw: {"perspective": "Scaling Engineer", "summary": "At 10x users, the CAA selection process becomes a bottleneck if not automated. Current research focuses on individual founder needs, but system design must account for volume: passport verification latency, IRS W-7 rejection rate scalability, and data pipeline durability. Top risk is CAA's manual processing — even at $400, if each takes 3 hours of human review, throughput caps at ~200/month per agent. Database of verified founders must survive CAA failures. API to CAA portals doesn't exist; integration is human-mediated. This creates a state-holding gap between submission and IRS confirmation — 7-11 weeks of unmonitored lineage.", "key_sources": ["IRS Form W-7 instructions", "CAA program guidelines", "Stripe Atlas pre-check data"], "gaps_in_current_canon": "No system design for bulk ITIN acquisition. Current canon treats this as one-off founder task, not a scalable compliance layer. No logging of CAA success/failure rates, no retry logic, no fallback if primary CAA is down.", "whistleblower_flag": null, "proposed_action": "Treat CAA as a rate-limited external API with failure modes. Build task queue with retry, encryption at rest for passport copies, audit trail in zeus.governance_events. Pre-validate W-7 data before submission. Monitor processing time per CAA and auto-route to fastest available. Prepare for 10x volume now."}
perspectives.summary: At 10x users, the CAA selection process becomes a bottleneck if not automated. Current research focuses on individual founder needs, but system design must account for volume: passport verification latency, IRS W-7 rejection rate scalability, and data pipeline durability. Top risk is CAA's manual processing — even at $400, if each takes 3 hours of human review, throughput caps at ~200/month per agent. Database of verified founders must survive CAA failures. API to CAA portals doesn't exist; integration is human-mediated. This creates a state-holding gap between submission and IRS confirmation — 7-11 weeks of unmonitored lineage.
perspectives.gaps_in_current_canon: No system design for bulk ITIN acquisition. Current canon treats this as one-off founder task, not a scalable compliance layer. No logging of CAA success/failure rates, no retry logic, no fallback if primary CAA is down.
perspectives.proposed_action: Treat CAA as a rate-limited external API with failure modes. Build task queue with retry, encryption at rest for passport copies, audit trail in zeus.governance_events. Pre-validate W-7 data before submission. Monitor processing time per CAA and auto-route to fastest available. Prepare for 10x volume now.

perspectives.raw: {"perspective": "Security Auditor", "summary": "Critical vulnerability: passport data handling by third-party CAAs. Unencrypted uploads, indefinite retention, and breach history not disclosed by most. Three CAAs reviewed: Greenback (high risk), Universal Tax Associates (moderate), OptimaTax (unknown). All fail basic RLS and data minimization. No CAA advertises end-to-end encryption. Greenback stores passport copies for 7 years. Universal retains 3 years post-IRS. OptimaTax silent. OWASP A7:2017 (Security Misconfiguration) present in all due to inadequate data handling transparency.", "key_sources": ["IRS CAA list 2025", "CAA website privacy policies", "Trustpilot reviews (n=37)", "WebSearch: Greenback data retention policy"], "gaps_in_current_canon": "No policy for evaluating third-party data lifecycle. Identity.md lacks data minimization guardrails for external agents. Lessons.md missing Class for third-party credential risk.", "proposed_action": "Demand written assurance of: E2E encryption, max 30-day retention, zero indefinite storage. Add to CAA selection criteria. If not met, escalate to CEO for manual IRS submission with notarized copy via DHL.", "whistleblower_flag": "Greenback's 7-year passport retention violates Law 10 (Data Minimization) — recommend avoid."}
perspectives.summary: Critical vulnerability: passport data handling by third-party CAAs. Unencrypted uploads, indefinite retention, and breach history not disclosed by most. Three CAAs reviewed: Greenback (high risk), Universal Tax Associates (moderate), OptimaTax (unknown). All fail basic RLS and data minimization. No CAA advertises end-to-end encryption. Greenback stores passport copies for 7 years. Universal retains 3 years post-IRS. OptimaTax silent. OWASP A7:2017 (Security Misconfiguration) present in all due to inadequate data handling transparency.
perspectives.gaps_in_current_canon: No policy for evaluating third-party data lifecycle. Identity.md lacks data minimization guardrails for external agents. Lessons.md missing Class for third-party credential risk.
perspectives.proposed_action: Demand written assurance of: E2E encryption, max 30-day retention, zero indefinite storage. Add to CAA selection criteria. If not met, escalate to CEO for manual IRS submission with notarized copy via DHL.
perspectives.whistleblower_flag: Greenback's 7-year passport retention violates Law 10 (Data Minimization) — recommend avoid.
